sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Allstate Corp Grows Position in Equinix, Inc. $EQIX
feed.summary: Allstate Corp significantly increased its stake in Equinix, Inc. by 162.8% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 5,526 shares valued at approximately $4.33 million. This increase occurred despite recent insider selling by Equinix executives, who sold shares totaling $6.62 million in the last 90 days. Equinix continues to demonstrate strong financial performance with beat quarterly EPS estimates and a 5.2% YoY revenue rise, alongside a quarterly dividend payment.

feed.title: International Paper declares quarterly dividend of $0.4625 per share
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE:IP) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.4625 per share for its common stock, payable on March 17, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 23. The company also declared a $1.00 per share dividend for its cumulative $4.00 preferred stock for the same period. Headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee, and with EMEA operations in London, International Paper reported $18.6 billion in net sales for 2024 and expanded its market presence in 2025 by acquiring DS Smith.

feed.title: FFIV Investors Have Opportunity to Lead F5, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) for alleged securities fraud, encouraging investors who purchased shares between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, to join before February 17, 2026. The lawsuit claims F5 made false statements about its security and customer fulfillment capabilities, particularly after a security incident, which led to investor losses. Investors can contact the firm to discuss their rights and participate in the lawsuit.

feed.title: KKR Increases Ownership Stake in Altavair
feed.summary: KKR, a global investment firm, has announced a definitive agreement to increase its ownership stake in Altavair and its sister company, AV AirFinance. This investment, funded from KKR's balance sheet, deepens the strategic partnership that began in 2018, during which KKR-managed funds have committed over $5 billion to aircraft leasing and lending. The move reinforces KKR's commitment to the aviation sector and Altavair's growth in the commercial aircraft leasing market, with key leadership promotions within Altavair to support its expansion.

feed.title: AIG collabs with CVC to invest $3.5B in private equity
feed.summary: AIG is partnering with CVC to invest $3.5 billion in private equity. This collaboration highlights AIG's strategic move to allocate a significant portion of capital towards private market investments.

feed.title: Dominion cleared to resume work on 2.6-GW Coastal Virginia project
feed.summary: Dominion has received clearance to continue construction on its 2.6 GW Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project. This development follows a court decision lifting an earlier construction ban, allowing the utility to proceed with the significant renewable energy initiative in the US.

feed.title: HBT Financial (HBT) Expected to Announce Quarterly Earnings on Monday
feed.summary: HBT Financial (NASDAQ: HBT) is scheduled to announce its Q4 2025 earnings before the market opens on Monday, January 26th. Analysts anticipate an EPS of $0.64 and revenue of approximately $61.0 million, with the earnings call set for 9:30 AM ET. The company recently authorized a $30.0 million share buyback and paid a quarterly dividend of $0.21, reflecting a focus on shareholder returns.

feed.title: PG&E to test equipment damaged in December fire during planned Civic Center outage
feed.summary: PG&E has scheduled a planned outage for the Civic Center area, starting shortly after midnight on Monday, to test equipment damaged in a December fire at its Mission Substation. This outage is expected to last up to 12 hours. Another shorter outage is planned for Tuesday in the Richmond District, affecting about 14,000 customers.

feed.title: New loyalty, 3 brands: Royal Caribbean Group launches flexible Points Choice program
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has introduced a new Points Choice reward initiative that allows cruisers to earn points across its three brands—RCI, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea—and redeem them on the loyalty program of their choice. This program builds on their existing Status Match program, enabling members to have equivalent tier status across all brands and providing greater flexibility and choice for guests and travel advisors. Points Choice will be available for sailings departing on or after January 26, 2026.

feed.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) Given Consensus Recommendation of "Hold" by Analysts
feed.summary: Nineteen analysts have issued a consensus "Hold" rating for Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO), with an average 12-month price target of $98.67. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating expectations with an EPS of $1.05 and revenue of $1.55 billion, a 35.4% increase year-over-year. Brown & Brown also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.165, resulting in an annualized dividend of $0.66 and a yield of approximately 0.8%.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) Given Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Analysts
feed.summary: Analysts have given Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average 12-month target price of $150.41, based on recommendations from twenty-three brokerages. The company recently exceeded Q3 earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $2.52 EPS against an expected $2.14, and $33.69 billion in revenue compared to an anticipated $32.29 billion. Despite positive analyst sentiment and strong performance, recent insider selling of approximately $11.96 million in shares has occurred over the past 90 days.

feed.title: AIG and CVC Announce Strategic Partnership
feed.summary: American International Group, Inc. (AIG) and CVC have formed a strategic partnership focusing on AIG's long-term investment objectives, leveraging CVC's expertise in insurance solutions and private markets. This collaboration includes the establishment of large-scale separately managed accounts (SMAs) across CVC's credit strategies and the launch of CVC's private equity secondaries evergreen platform, with AIG as a cornerstone investor. AIG will contribute up to $1.5 billion from its existing private equity portfolio to the secondaries platform and allocate up to $2 billion to SMAs managed by CVC, showcasing a shared ambition for a long-term relationship centered on scale, alignment, and bespoke solutions for global institutional and private wealth investors.

feed.title: International Paper Declares Quarterly Dividends Following DS Smith Acquisition
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) has declared quarterly dividends for its common and preferred shares, signaling confidence after its acquisition of DS Smith. The company will pay $0.4625 per common share and $1.00 per preferred share on March 17, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 23, 2026. This move highlights International Paper's commitment to returning capital to shareholders and its strong position in sustainable packaging markets.

feed.title: Judge Says Dominion Energy Can Resume Wind Project U.S. Halted
feed.summary: A federal judge has ruled that Dominion Energy Inc. can resume construction of its $11 billion wind project off the coast of Virginia, blocking a Trump administration order to stop the development. This decision marks the third such ruling this week, allowing halted wind projects to proceed while legal challenges against the U.S. Department of the Interior continue. Dominion Energy argued it was incurring significant daily losses due to the stoppage, and the judge found the government's national security concerns insufficient to justify further delays.

feed.title: Mosaic Issues Market Update And Preliminary Fourth Quarter 2025 Sales Results
feed.summary: The Mosaic Company provided a market update and preliminary fourth-quarter 2025 sales results, indicating challenging conditions due to weak North American fertilizer demand and intensifying credit constraints in Brazil. Despite the weak quarter, the company anticipates a more constructive 2026 with expected nutrient replenishment, government support for spring applications, firm prices for global demand, and stable pricing in the potash markets. Mosaic is scheduled to release its full fourth-quarter 2025 earnings results on February 24, 2026, followed by a conference call.

feed.title: Federal wage resources give Dollar General workers clear complaint pathway
feed.summary: The U.S. Department of Labor's Wage and Hour Division (WHD) offers comprehensive resources for Dollar General employees to address issues like unpaid wages and overtime. These tools include guidance on filing complaints, fact sheets on the Fair Labor Standards Act, and a system to claim recovered wages. The WHD aims to empower workers and encourage employers to improve payroll compliance.

feed.title: Boston Scientific to acquire Penumbra, expanding cardiovascular portfolio
feed.summary: Boston Scientific Corp. has agreed to acquire Penumbra, Inc. for approximately $14.5 billion, enhancing Boston Scientific's vascular portfolio with Penumbra's technologies for treating conditions like strokes, pulmonary embolisms, and heart attacks. This acquisition is expected to expand global access to these innovative medical devices and contribute to Boston Scientific's revenue and margin growth. The deal will also add Penumbra's CEO, Adam Elsesser, to Boston Scientific's board of directors.

feed.title: Water.org Partners with Gap Inc., Amazon, Starbucks, and Ecolab to Launch Get Blue™, Advancing Water.org's Goal of Reaching 200 Million People by 2030
feed.summary: Water.org has partnered with Gap Inc., Amazon, Starbucks, and Ecolab to launch Get Blue™, a global initiative aimed at accelerating access to safe water and sanitation. This partnership seeks to align business leadership, consumer engagement, and capital with Water.org's market-driven solutions to reach 200 million people by 2030. The initiative will mobilize funding through corporate brands and enterprise partnerships, with proceeds supporting Water.org's solutions to help families finance their water and sanitation needs.
